title: Lesson 15: Mini Project — Build Your First Website

content.explanation:
🚀 You Are Now Building Real Websites

Congratulations — you’ve learned all the core building blocks of HTML:

  • Headings
  • Paragraphs
  • Links
  • Images
  • Lists
  • Navigation

🏗️ This is Your First Real Project

Instead of learning one tag at a time, you will now combine everything into a complete webpage.

This is exactly how real developers build websites — by combining small pieces into a full structure.

🎯 Project Goal

You will build a simple personal homepage with navigation, content, and structure.

syntax_breakdown.content:
🧪 Project Structure Overview

Your page must include:

  • A main heading (h1)
  • A navigation menu (ul + li + a)
  • An image (img)
  • A paragraph (p)
  • A list of skills (ul)

📌 Real Developer Thinking

👉 Websites are just structured blocks stacked together

👉 Every section has a purpose

editor_sandbox.starter_code: <!-- Cymor Code Learner 🚀 FINAL BEGINNER PROJECT -->

mini_challenge.instruction:
🎯 FINAL MISSION: Build your first complete website

You must include ALL of the following:

1. A full HTML structure (DOCTYPE, html, head, body)
2. Title: My First Website
3. Inside body:
   - <h1> with your name or brand
   - Navigation menu with 3 links (Home, About, Contact)
   - An image (use any placeholder image)
   - A paragraph describing yourself or your journey
   - A list of 3 skills you are learning

🔥 Combine everything you learned from Lessons 1–14

👉 This is your first real developer project
mini_challenge.hint:
Start with:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
<title>My First Website</title>
</head>
<body>

Then build step by step:
- h1
- nav menu (ul + li + a)
- img
- p
- ul skills

Finish with </body> and </html>

quiz_engine.question: What is the main purpose of this mini project?
options: To combine multiple HTML elements into a real webpage structure
quiz_engine.explanation_feedback: Correct 🎉 This project is about combining all your HTML knowledge to build a complete structured webpage like a real developer.

takeaways: Real websites are made by combining multiple HTML elements
Structure matters more than individual tags
Navigation, images, and text work together
This is your first full webpage project

cheat_sheet.developer_rule: Think in sections, not single tags
